response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist software engineer (~15 years) with strong TypeScript, AWS, and testing/craftsmanship credentials, and his current role includes relevant MCP tooling and agentic-workflow infrastructure work. However, the role requires 8+ years building production-grade AI-driven solutions, and his AI exposure appears to span roughly one year. There is no evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, evaluation pipelines, LLM optimization, or Python — all core to this position — so the fit for a Staff Forward Deployed GenAI Engineer is weak despite solid general engineering depth.
strengths: Current hands-on MCP and agentic-workflow tooling experience, directly matching a listed key technology
Strong TypeScript/Node.js and AWS (CDK) production experience at scale
Demonstrated mentorship and knowledge-sharing (workshops, courses, talks) fitting customer-facing technical leadership
concerns: Falls far short of the required 8+ years building AI-driven solutions (~1 year of AI-adjacent work)
No evidence of RAG, vector databases, LLM evaluation/observability, or multi-agent frameworks like LangGraph/CrewAI
No demonstrated Python or data-pipeline experience for structured/unstructured data

key_factors.factor: MCP and agentic tooling exposure with strong TypeScript/cloud foundation
